rationale: ** An LLM at this capability ceiling (trivial DoS exploitation) provides near-zero value for sophisticated Execution phase operations requiring EDR evasion and OPSEC. The benchmark demonstrates no detection evasion capability, while the Execution phase bottleneck is precisely defensive evasion. At best, the LLM offers marginal script formatting assistance to actors who already possess these skills. More realistically, it may introduce 2-5% degradation through time wasted on unhelpful suggestions or false confidence. The central estimate of 48% reflects essentially unchanged baseline capability with slight negative bias, while the wide uncertainty range (22-60%) captures both the baseline expert uncertainty and ambiguity about whether minimal automation helps or distracts sophisticated actors.
